posts.author: urn:li:organization:98086113
posts.commentary:
Commissioning is where the truth is revealed.
It’s the moment systems are tested, validated and proven ready for operation - and where installation quality becomes impossible to hide.

Commissioning is not a box-ticking exercise. It is the process that ensures systems are designed, inspected, tested and documented correctly before going live.

We’ve broken down the fundamentals in the carousel below and why commissioning should be considered long before the final stages of a project.

Because when commissioning begins, the quality of the workforce behind the installation quickly becomes clear.

posts.author: urn:li:organization:98086113
posts.commentary:
Data centre projects operate within tightly controlled delivery programmes where installation quality, documentation accuracy and sequencing discipline directly influence commissioning outcomes.

Electricians and MEP operatives working in these environments must understand more than their trade. They must be comfortable operating within structured project frameworks where:

- Installations must withstand commissioning scrutiny
- Documentation and testing are completed to exacting standards
- Clean-build disciplines are maintained throughout delivery
- Permit-to-work systems are strictly followed
- Installation decisions can have operational consequences

This level of awareness is what separates general construction labour from operatives who are truly prepared for mission-critical delivery.

Workforce competency is central to our deployment model. Every operative is selected not only for technical capability, but for their ability to operate effectively within the demands of data centre and critical infrastructure environments.

Because when commissioning begins, the standard of the workforce behind the installation becomes immediately visible.

Reach out to know more: info\@filmtekdatasolutions.com

posts.author: urn:li:organization:98086113
posts.commentary:
Modular Power Is Only Faster If Integration Is Managed

Modular power units are designed to accelerate data centre delivery. But when the module arrives on site, the work is only just beginning.

- Transport inspections.
- Access infrastructure.
- Electrical connections.
- Cabling installation.
- Mechanical ancillaries.
- Technical cleaning ahead of commissioning.

Too often these activities are split across multiple subcontractors, each introducing additional coordination, programme risk and potential delay.

As @[Gavin Bannerman](urn:li:person:y0E9qox69A), Technical Operations Manager at Filmtek Data Solutions, explains:

“There’s a difference between a module that’s been delivered and a module that’s been integrated.”

We bring these secondary works together under a single managed scope; delivering turnkey module integration through teams that understand the sequencing, standards and discipline required in mission-critical environments.

One team. One mobilisation. One point of accountability.

From the moment the module arrives on site to the point it is integrated and ready for commissioning.

Contact us to know more: info\@filmtekdatasolutions.com

As data centre construction continues to scale across EMEA, the demand for experienced electricians who understand mission-critical environments is growing rapidly.

These environments are different from typical commercial installations. Work takes place within highly structured programmes where:

 • Clean-build discipline is maintained throughout installation
 • Permit-to-work systems are strictly followed
 • Installations must stand up to commissioning scrutiny
 • Testing and documentation are completed to the highest standard
 • Close coordination with wider MEP and commissioning teams is essential

We are particularly interested in connecting with electricians who have experience working in data centres, plant rooms, white space environments, or other forms of critical infrastructure.

Over the coming months we will be supporting a number of projects across the region, and we are actively building a talent pipeline of electricians who take pride in operating within these professional, high-performance environments.

If that reflects your experience, we would welcome the opportunity to connect. Register your interest here: https://wkf.ms/49UFACP

posts.author: urn:li:organization:98086113
posts.commentary:
Workforce capability has become a critical variable in programme success.
For projects operating within live or near-live environments, labour cannot simply be sourced when required and expected to integrate. It must be planned, structured and aligned to the realities of mission-critical delivery.

Our workforce model is designed specifically around that requirement.
Our approach centres on three principles:

- Strategic Workforce Planning
Forecasting labour demand against key delivery milestones, including the L1–L5 commissioning lifecycle, ensuring the right capability is deployed when programme pressure is at its highest.

- Competency & Compliance Management
 Rigorous certification oversight, ECS/CSCS validation and supervision where required, ensuring operatives meet both statutory and client-specific standards.

- Structured Deployment
Technically competent teams capable of integrating into existing PMO and GC structures, supported by experienced site leads who understand sequencing, safety and delivery discipline.

The aim is straightforward: reduce programme risk, support critical path activities and provide scalable workforce capability when projects need it most.

In mission-critical construction, workforce solutions must be engineered. 

Reach out to know more: info\@filmtekdatasolutions.com

posts.author: urn:li:organization:98086113
urn:li:organization:98086113
posts.commentary:
The Invisible Risk in Modern Buildings

As buildings become more connected, the conversation around performance is evolving. It’s no longer limited to energy efficiency and aesthetics, our clients are asking how their physical environment supports digital resilience.

Electromagnetic transmission through glazing is now part of that discussion.
EMF window films offer a discreet way to reduce signal transmission through glass - often the most permeable element of the building envelope - while also providing glass containment benefits. For data centres, secure facilities, healthcare environments and high-spec commercial buildings, this is becoming a considered layer of protection rather than a specialist add-on.

We deliver high-performance EMF film solutions tailored to secure and mission-critical environments.

If you are reviewing building security, information protection or infrastructure resilience, it may be time to look at your glass differently.

Contact us for more information: info\@filmtekdatasolutions.com

posts.author: urn:li:organization:98086113
posts.commentary:
This week, our Technical Operations Manager, @[Gavin Bannerman](urn:li:person:y0E9qox69A), shared a perspective that clearly resonated within the data centre sector.

“It’s not a labour shortage. It’s a standards shortage.”

Across EMEA, data centre capacity is scaling at pace. Yet on many projects, the pressure is not simply about numbers on site, it is about whether the electrical workforce truly understands the environment in which they are operating.

There is a material difference between commercial installation and mission-critical delivery.

Between wiring a distribution board and wiring it to BS 7671 standard, achieving first-time inspection approval, and producing documentation that supports rather than delays commissioning.

Between pulling cable and understanding containment strategy, segregation requirements and the implications of design integrity within a Tier III facility.

Between completing a panel schedule and appreciating fault levels, discrimination studies and how each circuit integrates into a resilient power architecture where errors carry operational consequences.

Mission-critical projects do not tolerate ambiguity. They require technically competent, accountable electricians who understand not just how to install, but why the precision matters.

At FDS, our approach is built around deploying managed, qualified teams who are mission-critical literate and aligned to structured delivery environments.

As programme demands increase, so too must the standard of the workforce supporting them.

If your upcoming phases require electrical capability aligned to commissioning, compliance and clean handover, we welcome the discussion.
